comment: Refreshed under the exact premise-source provenance prompt. Every audit-premise is judged independently where present.
prompt_summary: For each Assumptions.lean declaration, decide whether it is an explicit paper/model assumption, a source theorem condition, a documented caveat, a partial-formalization boundary, not a paper assumption, or uncertain.
For every exact -- audit-premise entry, give an independent premise_judgments entry. Group-level approval is insufficient.
Use source_text_model_primitive, source_text, or paper_condition only for premises explicitly stated by the source; cite source_location.
Use derived_from_source_primitives only when the Lean development derives the premise from prior source primitives.

Displayed formulas, capacity equations, threshold identities, density/mass rows, source-row packages, certificates, and proof conveniences are not paper assumptions unless the source explicitly assumes them; otherwise mark partial_boundary or not_paper_assumption.

assumption_positive_match_rates.reason: The source model gives sellers floor(k g(theta)) ratings by time k and the large-deviation theorem is stated in the nondegenerate asymptotic sample-rate regime.
assumption_positive_match_rates.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex Rating accumulation model; appendix_theory.tex Lemma problessthan and Theorem 1 proof

hgHi : 0 < sampleRate hi.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex: sellers receive n_k(theta)=floor(k g(theta)) matches/ratings; appendix_theory.tex: kg(theta_i) is the number of samples
hgHi : 0 < sampleRate hi.reason: The pairwise helper applies the paper's asymptotic sample-rate model to a compared high type; positivity is the nondegenerate domain in which the displayed large-deviation rate is meaningful.

hgLo : 0 < sampleRate lo.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex: sellers receive n_k(theta)=floor(k g(theta)) matches/ratings; appendix_theory.tex: kg(theta_i) is the number of samples
hgLo : 0 < sampleRate lo.reason: The pairwise helper applies the paper's asymptotic sample-rate model to a compared low type; positivity is the nondegenerate domain in which the displayed large-deviation rate is meaningful.

hpositive_sample : ∀ θ : Fin n, 0 < samp.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex Rating accumulation model; appendix_theory.tex Theorem 1 finite ordered type formula
hpositive_sample : ∀ θ : Fin n, 0 < samp.reason: The finite-chain endpoint applies the same positive sample-rate convention uniformly across the finite seller-type chain used in Theorem 1.

assumption_positive_integer_sample_rates.reason: Integer-rate helper rows instantiate the paper's positive asymptotic sample-rate model with positive natural sample rates.
assumption_positive_integer_sample_rates.source_location: Appendix C finite/integer-rate specialization of floor(k g(theta)) sample counts

hgHi : 0 < gHi.source_location: Derived from the positive sample-rate condition for the integer-rate specialization of n_k(theta)=floor(k g(theta))
hgHi : 0 < gHi.reason: The natural-valued helper is a representation bridge; positive integer rates are the integer instance of the source positive sample-rate condition.

hgLo : 0 < gLo.source_location: Derived from the positive sample-rate condition for the integer-rate specialization of n_k(theta)=floor(k g(theta))
hgLo : 0 < gLo.reason: The natural-valued helper is a representation bridge; positive integer rates are the integer instance of the source positive sample-rate condition.

assumption_nonpositive_common_dual_param.reason: The common-dual signs are lower-tail Chernoff/Laplace-principle witnesses for the source pairwise score-comparison event; the main support-safe endpoint derives the needed witness package internally.
assumption_nonpositive_common_dual_param.source_location: appendix_theory.tex Lemma problessthan proof and Theorem 1 variational formula

hz : z ≤ 0.source_location: Derived by the source lower-tail large-deviation route for x_k(theta_hi)-x_k(theta_lo) <= 0
hz : z ≤ 0.reason: This is not an extra model assumption. It is the sign of the common-dual witness exposed by an auxiliary helper; the canonical endpoint packages/derives the witness from the source variational problem.

hz : ∀ p : finiteChainOrderedPair n, z p.source_location: Finite-chain pointwise version of the source lower-tail large-deviation route
hz : ∀ p : finiteChainOrderedPair n, z p.reason: This is the finite ordered-chain form of the same lower-tail common-dual witness condition, exposed for helper reuse rather than assumed by the canonical source endpoint.

assumption_pairwise_threshold_minimizer.reason: The paper's displayed rate uses an infimum over the common threshold a; helper rows expose a minimizer witness while the main endpoint works through the support-safe adjacent threshold-rate minimum.
assumption_pairwise_threshold_minimizer.source_location: appendix_theory.tex Lemma problessthan; adjectivesmodel.tex Theorem 1 equation rW

hthreshold_min : ∀ b : ℝ, M.pairwiseRate.source_location: Derived from the paper's inf_a pairwise objective together with the finite support-safe threshold-rate endpoint
hthreshold_min : ∀ b : ℝ, M.pairwiseRate.reason: The premise says the selected threshold realizes the source displayed infimum; it is a helper-level witness, not an independent paper assumption.

assumption_two_sided_score_witnesses.reason: Finite-support lower-bound helper rows expose positive and negative score-gap witnesses; the main finite ordinal endpoint derives them from full rating-law support and a strict score span.
assumption_two_sided_score_witnesses.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex ordered finite rating scale, strictly decreasing cumulative rating law, and increasing score design

hscorePos : 0 < twoSampleRateBlockScore.source_location: Derived in Lean from finite ordinal full support and score span for the source rating model
hscorePos : 0 < twoSampleRateBlockScore.reason: The positive score-gap sample is obtained from the bottom/top rating atoms supplied by the source finite ordinal support convention.

hscoreNeg : twoSampleRateBlockScore M gH.source_location: Derived in Lean from finite ordinal full support and score span for the source rating model
hscoreNeg : twoSampleRateBlockScore M gH.reason: The negative score-gap sample is obtained from the bottom/top rating atoms supplied by the source finite ordinal support convention.

assumption_monotone_rating_scores.reason: The source design problem optimizes over increasing score functions on the ordered rating scale.
assumption_monotone_rating_scores.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex design optimization paragraph after Theorem 1

hscore_mono : Monotone M.score.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex: choose a random, increasing set of scores phi(y) in [0,1]
hscore_mono : Monotone M.score.reason: Lean's monotone score condition is the finite ordinal representation of the paper's increasing score functions.

assumption_full_finite_ordinal_rating_su.reason: The source has a finite ordered rating scale and assumes the cumulative rating law is strictly decreasing in rating level; this supplies the positive finite rating support used by the support-safe endpoint.
assumption_full_finite_ordinal_rating_su.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex Ratings model: finite ordered Y, rho(theta,y|Y), R(theta,y|Y), and R strictly decreasing with y

hfull_support : M.fullSupport.source_location: Derived from the source finite ordered rating scale and strict decrease of R(theta,y|Y) in y
hfull_support : M.fullSupport.reason: Strictly decreasing cumulative probabilities across the finite rating order imply positive mass at the represented rating levels used by the canonical finite ordinal endpoint.

assumption_score_range_and_strict_span.reason: The source score function maps ordered rating levels into [0,1] and the optimization uses increasing score functions, giving lower/upper bounds and a nontrivial span.
assumption_score_range_and_strict_span.source_location: adjectivesmodel.tex Ratings model and design optimization paragraph

hscore_low_le : ∀ r : Rating, M.score rL.source_location: Derived from the source ordered finite rating scale and increasing score function phi
hscore_low_le : ∀ r : Rating, M.score rL.reason: The selected lowest rating level is below every other rating in the ordinal score order.

hscore_le_high : ∀ r : Rating, M.score r.source_location: Derived from the source ordered finite rating scale and increasing score function phi
hscore_le_high : ∀ r : Rating, M.score r.reason: The selected highest rating level is above every other rating in the ordinal score order.

hscore_lt : M.score rLow < M.score rHigh.source_location: Derived from the source nontrivial finite rating scale and increasing score design phi(y) in [0,1]
hscore_lt : M.score rLow < M.score rHigh.reason: The strict span records the nonconstant increasing-score regime used for ranking informativeness.
